NEOW.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.firs:
[sine]......?
An unfamiliar touch... is this your first time here...?[/sine]

Yes. I fell into this place
while asleep on my anvil. I'm looking for the way [gold]home[/gold].
[sine]The way home... with but a single [gold]hammer[/gold] in your hand?[/sine]

One hammer is enough.
[gold]Forging[/gold] always begins with what is already in hand.

[sine]Very well... then strike.
I shall watch what is engraved upon your [gold]anvil[/gold].[/sine]
[sine]A touch from another world... how long it has been...[/sine]
...Just point me the way home.
One [gold]hammer[/gold] is all I need.
[sine]Returned again, child of the hammer...
Has your [gold]anvil[/gold] grown cold?[/sine]
No time for it to cool.
The next [gold]forging[/gold] is already waiting.
[sine]A hammer's clang stirs me from sleep...
It has been so long...[/sine]
Sorry if I was loud.
I haven't learned a quiet way to strike yet.
[sine]Your hands are slowly carving the [gold]way home[/gold]...[/sine]
Carved or chipped, it's fine.
It's all [gold]forging[/gold] in the end.
Oh, a guest with a hammer!
Piles of useful [gold]parts[/gold] just for you!
Let me see what you've got.
Whatever's rough, I can always [gold]forge[/gold] into shape.
Ooh, back again, smith!
Fresh [gold]goods[/gold] came in today!
You keep looking out for me.
Thanks, again.
You've become a regular!
Let's strike a special [gold]deal[/gold] today!
I do like a special deal.
Long as it's something the hammer can use.
Friend! Today I might show you my [gold]secret[/gold]!
We're friends now?
Then I'd better take a proper look.
[thinky_dots]Your hammer... it stirs the [gold]ember[/gold] beneath my scales...[/thinky_dots]
...Fire-tenders deserve some respect.
Mind lending me a bit of your [gold]heat[/gold]?
[thinky_dots]Your [gold]heat[/gold] has not scattered...
You tend it well.[/thinky_dots]
Less tending, more walking together.
Fire dies when you cage it.
[thinky_dots]My scales are drying...
Could your hammer cool them?[/thinky_dots]
I'll strike gently if you need it.
Cooling work shouldn't be hit too hard.
[thinky_dots]Smith, your [gold]ember[/gold] is now as deep as my scales.[/thinky_dots]
...That's generous.
I'm still on the anvil, you know.
OROBAS.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-:
Too hard! Too hot!
That hammer... scary! Very scary!!

Easy, I won't swing it.
Just came to ask the way.
OROBAS.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.1-:
Again! That hammer again!
Scary! Very scary!

Same hammer as before.
I won't swing it, easy now.
OROBAS.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.2-:
Friend or guest, I won't hurt you.
I'll promise that.
OROBAS.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.3-:
Hammer friend!
Come back again, even better!

Glad to hear it.
Don't lose your way on the road back.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-0:
An otherworldly [sine]smith[/sine], standing before me.
The road home does not lie at the end of that hammer.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-1:
...Then where is it?
Any [gold]answer[/gold] worth carrying, I'll take it.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.1-0:
[sine]The hammer is no answer.
Yet you keep striking.[/sine]
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.1-1:
Even if it isn't, the hammer is what fits my hand.
Not reason enough to put it down.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.2-1:
If it wavered, I couldn't strike.
You make nothing on a shaking anvil.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.3-0: [sine]Then how far will your hammer reach?[/sine]
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.3-1:
As far as it can.
That's the answer of [gold]forging[/gold].
TEZCATARA.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER:
What a [jitter][b]hot[/b][/jitter] guest! The kiln is always open.
Mind lending some of your [gold]heat[/gold]?

My heat is meant for the hammer.
You wouldn't send me off empty-handed, right?

My [jitter]hot[/jitter] guest is back!
The kiln was [b]waiting[/b] for you!

Sweet of you to wait, but it's a bit too hot here.
I'll stand back a step.

More! More! Give me your [gold]heat[/gold]!
Take all you want, give it all!

If I give it all, I go cold.
Sharing in halves is more like a trade, isn't it?

...Good. I've never met such a [b]calm[/b] guest.
Name your price.

Not calm, just minding my own heat.
That's why I trade slowly.
Quite the hammer, smith!
But true weapons grow from [gold]beasts[/gold]!
That's your way. Not mine.
My work is born on my [gold]anvil[/gold].
Back again, smith!
Will you spar with my [gold]beasts[/gold] today!
I'll pass on the beasts.
No good for either of us, getting hurt.
Hmph, smith!
I'll give you that [gold]hammer[/gold] of yours, at least!
Thanks.
Your beasts look well kept, too.
Next time, I want to test your hammer [b]for real[/b]!
A test is fine, but no real injuries.
For both of us.
NONUPEIPE.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER:
Ah, a visitor with a hammer.
May today's [gold]dice[/gold] favor you.

Wouldn't have brought a hammer if I trusted luck.
Still, I'll roll once.

Back again, smith.
Which way will [gold]fortune[/gold] flow today?

We'll find out when it flows.
I'll keep hammering in the meantime.

Your [gold]dice[/gold] always come up even.
You never lean on luck.

What's at hand is just more reliable.
Though, when a roll is called for, I'll roll.

Guests like you are rare.
The [gold]odds[/gold] of meeting again are high, aren't they?

High or low, if I'm passing through, I'll stop by.
It was a good meeting.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARA:
So, you climbed all the way up here with a single hammer.
Pioneer of an unfamiliar [gold]world[/gold].

...I won't drag this out.
Open the [gold]door[/gold] home, and I'll wrap this up clean.

The door is already beneath your feet.
The only question is whether you are worthy to [b]pay the price[/b].

Prices always get paid on the [gold]anvil[/gold].
Today's no different.

Very well, smith.
Let a single [jitter]hammer strike[/jitter] settle it.

You again, hammer-bearer.
You could have gone home. Why return?

[gold]Forging[/gold] never ends in one strike.
Keep hammering till the shape is right.

By now my face must be familiar.
What do you come to hammer today?

Today it's [gold]you[/gold] on the anvil.
Ready?

The [b]smith's song[/b] rings here again.
Come, one last strike.
